phrases.intent_en: Ask which platform the TGV to Paris departs from
phrases.text_local: De quel quai part le TGV pour Paris, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: duh kel KAY par luh tay-zhay-VAY poor pah-REE, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Lyon Part-Dieu is France's second busiest station. TGVs to Paris Saint-Exupéry Airport and Paris Gare de Lyon depart frequently, with journey times of around two hours.

responses.english: Check the display board. Platforms vary by schedule.
responses.french: Vérifiez l'affichage. Les quais varient selon l'horaire.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to validate a train ticket
phrases.text_local: Où faut-il composter mon billet avant de monter dans le train ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo foh-TEEL kohm-pos-TAY mohn bee-YEH ah-VAHN duh mohn-TAY dahn luh TREHN
phrases.cultural_note: All French train tickets must be composted (validated) in the yellow machines on the platform before boarding. Failing to do so can result on-the-spot fines even with a valid ticket.

responses.english: It's no longer necessary with electronic tickets.
responses.phonetic: suh neh PLOO nay-seh-SEHR ah-VEK lay bee-YEH ay-lek-troh-NEEK
responses.french: Ce n'est plus nécessaire avec les billets électroniques.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find left luggage
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve la consigne à bagages, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah kohn-SEENY ah bah-GAZH, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Left luggage (consigne) is available at the station. Automated lockers and staffed storage are both options. This is useful if you want to explore the city before your train.

responses.english: On level zero, near the south exit.
responses.phonetic: oh nee-VOH ZAY-roh, preh duh lah sor-TEE SOOD
responses.french: Au niveau zéro, près de la sortie sud.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about connections to the tram network
phrases.text_local: Comment rejoindre le réseau de tramway depuis la gare ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN ruh-ZHWANDR luh ray-ZOH duh tram-WAY duh-PWEE lah GAR
phrases.cultural_note: Lyon has an extensive tram network. Tram lines T1, T3, and T4 stop directly in front of Gare Part-Dieu, making onward connections straightforward.

responses.english: The tram stop is in front of the station.
responses.phonetic: lah stah-SYOHN duh TRAM eh duh-VAHN lah GAR
responses.french: La station de tram est devant la gare.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to buy a ticket to Lyon Saint-Exupéry Airport
phrases.text_local: Où acheter un billet pour l'aéroport Saint-Exupéry ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo ash-TAY uhn bee-YEH poor lay-roh-POR sahn-teg-zoo-pay-REE
phrases.cultural_note: The Rhônexpress tram-train connects Lyon Part-Dieu to Lyon Saint-Exupéry Airport in about 30 minutes. Tickets can be bought at the Rhônexpress desks or online.

responses.english: At the counter or the machine.
